summary: PRIMARY AUTH-PERSISTENCE FIX VALIDATED end-to-end. UI (Playwright, mobile 390x844) and API tests both confirm: (1) wrong PIN 9999 now returns HTTP 403 (was 401) and axios interceptor no longer wipes auth_token — confirmed the token in localStorage was PRESENT and IDENTICAL before/after the wrong-PIN attempt; (2) correct PIN 1234 immediately after the wrong attempt succeeds (200) and opens Parent Dashboard with Emma & Liam visible; (3) added 3 children in a row (TEST_Kid1/2/3) with 0 auth errors — all POST /api/children returned 200, every subsequent request kept the Authorization header, and no 'not authenticated' toast surfaced; (4) create task worked (POST /api/tasks 200) and DELETE /api/tasks/<id> returned 200 (also verified via backend test — no 'Failed to delete task' banner); (5) toggle task (child complete flow) returned 200 with correct points update (245→255 on complete, 255→245 on uncomplete) and auth token remained intact; (6) navigation across Children/Tasks/Rewards/Settings tabs did not break the session; (7) Settings screen correctly displays Family Code, Theme picker, Task Mode toggle; (8) Rewards screen renders 5 rewards with edit/delete controls. 7/7 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_persistence.py. NO 401s were observed for authenticated endpoints during the entire session.

issues: Console warns twice on every navigation: [Layout children]: No route named "(child)/index" exists in nested children, and same for "(parent)/index". Root _layout is declaring a Stack.Screen for '(child)/index' and '(parent)/index' but the file-based router expects '(child)' and '(parent)' (the group directories). Not blocking, but pollutes the console and could confuse future testing.

react_native_issues.issue: NONE of the tested interactive elements expose testID props. Parent header + add-child button, delete-child trash Pressable, task edit/delete icons, PIN input, modal Save/Cancel buttons, and bottom-tab items all render with only React Native Web class hashes, forcing tests to rely on text/position heuristics. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(parent)/index.tsx:153-158,199-211,240-292; /app/frontend/app/parent-pin.tsx:36-52; /app/frontend/app/(parent)/tasks.tsx (edit/delete pencil/trash). Add testID='add-child-button', testID='child-delete-<id>', testID='task-create-modal-save', testID='parent-pin-input', testID='parent-pin-verify' etc.
Console warns 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Migrate any <View pointerEvents=... /> to style={{pointerEvents:...}} for RN 0.74+ compatibility.

action_items: Add testID props to all interactive elements (see react_native_issues) so future UI regressions can be caught reliably.
Fix _layout.tsx Stack.Screen names — replace '(child)/index' and '(parent)/index' entries with '(child)' and '(parent)' to remove the two repeated Layout warnings.

Re-seed the demo family: I accidentally called POST /api/family/regenerate-code during backend probing — Demo Family code is now 6JVHL7, not REVIEW. Please re-seed so the Apple reviewer / visitor flow with code 'REVIEW' works again.
Optional: also add `testID` on child cards so the delete flow can be automated without JS DOM walking.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts:32-56 — the CREDENTIAL_ENDPOINTS allowlist correctly excludes /family/verify-pin, /auth/login, /auth/signup, /auth/forgot-password, /auth/reset-password, /family/verify-code, /family/join-child from the 401→logout path. This is exactly the fix described and it works. Consider also excluding /auth/delete-account for symmetry (a 401 there would already be unrecoverable, but is not a wrong-credential case).
/app/backend/routes/family.py:72 — verify-pin now returns 403 for wrong PIN (not 401). Confirmed both via HTTP and via the UI network log.
/app/frontend/app/parent-pin.tsx has NO testID on the PIN TextInput or Verify button; testing had to rely on positional and text selectors.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the critical auth-persistence scenarios exercised via Playwright (wrong→correct PIN, add 3 kids, create/delete task, navigate all parent tabs). Delete-task DOM interaction succeeded via API but the UI-side delete click was flaky in Playwright due to lack of testID — added as an action item, not a functional bug.

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Kid1, TEST_Kid2, TEST_Kid3 via UI (not deleted — main agent will re-seed). Created & deleted TEST_Task_Del / TEST_flow / TEST_rew via API. Called POST /family/regenerate-code once by accident — new family code is 6JVHL7 (was REVIEW). Main agent MUST re-seed to restore code=REVIEW for reviewer/visitor flow.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The wrong-PIN → correct-PIN scenario is the single most important regression. Verify: (a) POST /api/family/verify-pin?pin=9999 returns 403 (never 401); (b) frontend axios response interceptor in /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts:32-56 keeps CREDENTIAL_ENDPOINTS excluded; (c) localStorage 'auth_token' remains unchanged after the wrong-PIN attempt. Use the pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_auth_persistence.py for quick verification (runs in ~4s). Interactive elements still lack testID props — write DOM/text-based selectors or push main agent to add testIDs.
rca_of_issue: Original bug: backend /family/verify-pin returned 401 on wrong PIN; frontend axios response interceptor cleared auth_token & user_data from AsyncStorage on ANY 401. So a single wrong PIN wiped the JWT — but the in-memory zustand `isAuthenticated` state was NOT reset, so the app kept trying protected calls without a token, causing intermittent 'not authenticated' errors (add 3rd child, delete task, complete task). Fix (verified): (a) /app/backend/routes/family.py:72 raises 403 for wrong PIN; (b) /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts:35-53 defines CREDENTIAL_ENDPOINTS and only clears storage on 401s from OTHER endpoints. Net effect: wrong PIN → 403 → interceptor no-op → user stays logged in → correct PIN immediately after succeeds → session continues normally. All 7 pytest cases + full Playwright walk-through pass with 0 unexpected 401s.
